name: Infrastructure / IaC Baseline Pack
purpose: Expectations for provisioning infrastructure as code, so applies are reproducible, least-privilege, policy-compliant, and blast-radius-controlled.
whenToApply: The change provisions or modifies infrastructure via code (Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation, Kubernetes manifests).
IAM, networking, or stateful resources are affected.
requiredCapabilities: Reproducible, idempotent apply: plan is reviewed and applied without manual drift.
Drift detection between declared and actual state, with alerting.
Least-privilege IAM: no wildcard admin; scoped roles and policies.
Policy-as-code checks (e.g. OPA/Conftest) enforce guardrails before apply.
No hardcoded secrets in IaC; secrets sourced from a manager.
recommendedCapabilities: Automated plan on PR with human approval to apply.
metadataRequirements: Resource inventory with owners and environments.
qualityGates: Apply is reproducible and idempotent; a re-plan shows no unexpected drift.
Policy-as-code checks pass; no wildcard-admin IAM.
State is remote, locked, and versioned.
Resources are tagged for ownership and cost; a destroy/rollback path is known.
testingExpectations: Plan review and policy checks in CI.
documentationExpectations: Document the resource inventory, IAM model, and state backend.
Document environment topology and blast-radius boundaries.

implementationIssueTemplates.name: Provision or Change Infrastructure
implementationIssueTemplates.description: Modify infrastructure via code under the IaC gates.
implementationIssueTemplates.template:
## Goal
Describe the resource change and environments affected.

## Baseline Packs
- technology/infrastructure

## Required Evidence
- Reviewed plan; policy-as-code pass
- Least-privilege IAM diff
- Secret scan clean
- Rollback/destroy path

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reproducible apply, no unexpected drift
- Policies pass; no wildcard admin
- State remote/locked; tagged
